prose:
Frontier-orbital single-point calculation on TEMPO (2,2,6,6-tetramethylpiperidine-1-oxyl) using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier 'standard', density-fitted) in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 2 (open-shell doublet, UKS). Geometry: user-supplied .xyz at /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/tempo/tempo.xyz; no re-optimization (single point on supplied geometry). SCF converged in 12 cycles. No warnings reported — clean run. Integrity check: trustworthy = true, all checks passed.

Headline result — HOMO–LUMO gap: 3.8724203 eV
HOMO (index 86): −6.3375439 eV
LUMO (index 87): −2.4651235 eV
Total electronic energy: −483.88366 Hartree (−13167.145 eV)
n_atoms = 29, n_orbitals = 898, n_occupied = 87

Koopmans descriptors: vertical IP = 6.3375 eV, vertical EA = 2.4651 eV, electronegativity = 4.4013 eV, chemical hardness = 1.9362 eV, chemical softness = 0.51647 eV⁻¹, electrophilicity index = 5.0025 eV.

These are Koopmans-style orbital eigenvalues (not measured IPs/EAs). The result is reported as B3LYP/def2-TZVP // supplied geometry.
